body_txt: Your friend reveals that they know of some weakness that you have, or has a tactic or piece of Applied Phlebotinum in reserve specifically to take you down; you know, " just in case ". Maybe: Whatever their motives, they're sitting on Hidden Supplies of the necessary items or substances to put you down in an instant if the need arises. Regardless of why the Betrayal Insurance policy was originally created, it's rare for it to be used. How you feel when you find out your friend has such a policy out on you will vary. Mostly depending on where the show your show falls on the Sliding Scale of Idealism Versus Cynicism . This also occurs when the betrayal has already happened and one of the betrayed reveals they have something for just this situation. Compare to Kryptonite Ring , where someone personally takes precautions in case they ever go bad themselves. Compare Morality Chain , another way to ensure the barely-good guy doesn't turn bad and betray the others; in rare cases this and that trope can overlap. See also Dead Man's Switch . Has nothing to do with an insurance company betraying their customers, usually by denying a claim and refusing to pay out.
